verses.text: These are the generations of Esau — who is Edom. He had taken three wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah daughter of Elon the Hittite, Aholibamah daughter of Anah, and Bashemath, Ishmael's daughter, sister of Nebajoth.

These are the generations of Esau — who is Edom. He had taken three wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah daughter of Elon the Hittite, Aholibamah daughter of Anah, and Bashemath, Ishmael's daughter, sister of Nebajoth.

These are the generations of Esau — who is Edom. He had taken three wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah daughter of Elon the Hittite, Aholibamah daughter of Anah, and Bashemath, Ishmael's daughter, sister of Nebajoth.
Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau; Bashemath bore Reuel; Aholibamah bore Jeush, Jaalam, and Korah. These are the sons of Esau born to him in the land of Canaan.
Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau; Bashemath bore Reuel; Aholibamah bore Jeush, Jaalam, and Korah. These are the sons of Esau born to him in the land of Canaan.

Esau took his wives, sons, daughters, livestock, and all his possessions and went to a country apart from Jacob — because their wealth was too great for the land to hold them both. He settled in Mount Seir. The brothers' peace holds because they no longer share borders.

Esau took his wives, sons, daughters, livestock, and all his possessions and went to a country apart from Jacob — because their wealth was too great for the land to hold them both. He settled in Mount Seir. The brothers' peace holds because they no longer share borders.

Esau took his wives, sons, daughters, livestock, and all his possessions and went to a country apart from Jacob — because their wealth was too great for the land to hold them both. He settled in Mount Seir. The brothers' peace holds because they no longer share borders.
These are the generations of Esau, father of the Edomites in Mount Seir. The names of his sons are repeated for the official record: Eliphaz son of Adah, Reuel son of Bashemath.
These are the generations of Esau, father of the Edomites in Mount Seir. The names of his sons are repeated for the official record: Eliphaz son of Adah, Reuel son of Bashemath.

Eliphaz's sons: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, Kenaz; and from his concubine Timna came Amalek — the ancestor of a people who will trouble Israel for centuries. Reuel's sons: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, Mizzah. Aholibamah bore Jeush, Jaalam, Korah.

Eliphaz's sons: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, Kenaz; and from his concubine Timna came Amalek — the ancestor of a people who will trouble Israel for centuries. Reuel's sons: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, Mizzah. Aholibamah bore Jeush, Jaalam, Korah.

Eliphaz's sons: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, Kenaz; and from his concubine Timna came Amalek — the ancestor of a people who will trouble Israel for centuries. Reuel's sons: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, Mizzah. Aholibamah bore Jeush, Jaalam, Korah.

Eliphaz's sons: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, Kenaz; and from his concubine Timna came Amalek — the ancestor of a people who will trouble Israel for centuries. Reuel's sons: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, Mizzah. Aholibamah bore Jeush, Jaalam, Korah.
These are the chiefs of Esau's sons. From Eliphaz, the firstborn: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz, Korah, Gatam, Amalek — chiefs of Eliphaz in Edom.
These are the chiefs of Esau's sons. From Eliphaz, the firstborn: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz, Korah, Gatam, Amalek — chiefs of Eliphaz in Edom.
From Reuel: chiefs Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, Mizzah. From Aholibamah: chiefs Jeush, Jaalam, Korah. These are the sons of Esau and their chiefs — that is, Edom.
From Reuel: chiefs Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, Mizzah. From Aholibamah: chiefs Jeush, Jaalam, Korah. These are the sons of Esau and their chiefs — that is, Edom.
From Reuel: chiefs Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, Mizzah. From Aholibamah: chiefs Jeush, Jaalam, Korah. These are the sons of Esau and their chiefs — that is, Edom.

Now the sons of Seir the Horite — the original inhabitants of the land before Esau came. Their dukes: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, Dishan. Lotan's sons: Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna — the same Timna who later became Eliphaz's concubine.

Now the sons of Seir the Horite — the original inhabitants of the land before Esau came. Their dukes: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, Dishan. Lotan's sons: Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna — the same Timna who later became Eliphaz's concubine.

Now the sons of Seir the Horite — the original inhabitants of the land before Esau came. Their dukes: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, Dishan. Lotan's sons: Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna — the same Timna who later became Eliphaz's concubine.

Shobal's sons: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, Onam. Zibeon's sons: Ajah and Anah — this is the Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness while pasturing his father's donkeys. Anah's children: Dishon, and Aholibamah.

Shobal's sons: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, Onam. Zibeon's sons: Ajah and Anah — this is the Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness while pasturing his father's donkeys. Anah's children: Dishon, and Aholibamah.

Shobal's sons: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, Onam. Zibeon's sons: Ajah and Anah — this is the Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness while pasturing his father's donkeys. Anah's children: Dishon, and Aholibamah.
Dishon's sons: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, Cheran. Ezer's sons: Bilhan, Zaavan, Akan. Dishan's: Uz and Aran. The Horite chiefs are accounted for in full.
Dishon's sons: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, Cheran. Ezer's sons: Bilhan, Zaavan, Akan. Dishan's: Uz and Aran. The Horite chiefs are accounted for in full.
Dishon's sons: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, Cheran. Ezer's sons: Bilhan, Zaavan, Akan. Dishan's: Uz and Aran. The Horite chiefs are accounted for in full.

These are the chiefs of the Horites: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, Dishan — chiefs among the Horites in the land of Seir. Two peoples lived together; eventually Esau's family absorbed them.

These are the chiefs of the Horites: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, Dishan — chiefs among the Horites in the land of Seir. Two peoples lived together; eventually Esau's family absorbed them.

These are the kings who reigned in Edom before any king ruled in Israel. Bela son of Beor reigned in Dinhabah. Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned after him. Husham of Teman reigned after Jobab. Edom had kings centuries before Israel did.

These are the kings who reigned in Edom before any king ruled in Israel. Bela son of Beor reigned in Dinhabah. Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned after him. Husham of Teman reigned after Jobab. Edom had kings centuries before Israel did.

These are the kings who reigned in Edom before any king ruled in Israel. Bela son of Beor reigned in Dinhabah. Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned after him. Husham of Teman reigned after Jobab. Edom had kings centuries before Israel did.

Hadad son of Bedad — who defeated Midian in the field of Moab — reigned at Avith. Samlah of Masrekah reigned next. Saul of Rehoboth-by-the-River next. Baal-hanan son of Achbor after him. Each succession is recorded — even of a rival nation, every name matters to God.

Hadad son of Bedad — who defeated Midian in the field of Moab — reigned at Avith. Samlah of Masrekah reigned next. Saul of Rehoboth-by-the-River next. Baal-hanan son of Achbor after him. Each succession is recorded — even of a rival nation, every name matters to God.

Hadad son of Bedad — who defeated Midian in the field of Moab — reigned at Avith. Samlah of Masrekah reigned next. Saul of Rehoboth-by-the-River next. Baal-hanan son of Achbor after him. Each succession is recorded — even of a rival nation, every name matters to God.

Hadad son of Bedad — who defeated Midian in the field of Moab — reigned at Avith. Samlah of Masrekah reigned next. Saul of Rehoboth-by-the-River next. Baal-hanan son of Achbor after him. Each succession is recorded — even of a rival nation, every name matters to God.
Baal-hanan died; Hadar reigned from Pau. His wife was Mehetabel, daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezahab. The text remembers wives and mothers all the way down.
Baal-hanan died; Hadar reigned from Pau. His wife was Mehetabel, daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezahab. The text remembers wives and mothers all the way down.

Final list of Esau's chiefs by their territories: Timnah, Alvah, Jetheth, Aholibamah, Elah, Pinon, Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar, Magdiel, Iram. These are the chiefs of Edom, by their settlements in the land of their possession. Esau is Edom — and his line is fully named.
